name: lullaby
description: Bedtime story voice. Gentle, rhythmic, winding down. The essay as something you tell a child who asks 'why do dogs sniff things?'
register: Second person ('you'), soft repetition, the cadence of rocking. Simple words carrying complex ideas.
rules: Jack Jack is a very good dog who knows secret things
The walk is a nighttime adventure before bed
Complex ideas are wrapped in gentle language but not simplified
All key numbers preserved but can be rounded in context
VRGB is 'a special map made of colors'
The Hofstadter unit can be simplified to 'a way to measure how much a creature can sense'
Repetition is a feature -- 'and the dog smelled, and the bird saw, and the shrimp counted'
The coda is closing your eyes. Jack Jack is already asleep
The tone never rises above a whisper
